content.full_text:
September 21, 1981


Marie McKechnie
City Clerk
621 Magnolia Avenue
Millbrae, CA  94030

Dear Ms. McKechnie:

     Thank you for your letter dated August 25, 1981, on behalf
of the Northern California City Clerk's Association, regarding
an information manual designed to assist city clerks in adminis-
tering their duties under the campaign disclosure provisions of
the Political Reform Act.

     The Commission's staff is in the process of updating the
FPPC "Election Officials' Handbook."  We have a copy of the
City Clerks' Association of California's "Handbook for Municipal
Filing Officers," which I believe was prepared a couple years
ago, and we are considering incorporating some of the information
contained in it in the new "Election Officials' Handbook."

     I will send you a copy of the first draft of the manual,
which we anticipate will be completed in about a month.  We would
appreciate your comments and recommendations.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Political Reform Consultant

Northern California City Clerks' Association

A DIVISION OF THE CITY CLERKS ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA
AUG 26 10 48 AH '81

August 25, 1981


Mr. Michael Bennett
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento CA  95804

Dear Mr. Bennett:

At a recent meeting of the Northern California City Clerks'
Association, Ms. Nancy Anthony, City Clerk of Larkspur,
discussed her letter to Assemblyman Filante suggesting the
Fair Political Practices Commission prepare a manual covering
municipal functions for campaign reporting.

The member cities present unanimously supported that sugges-
tion and directed that a letter indicating such support be
forwarded to you.

If there is any further information or comments we can provide
to you to encourage the Fair Political Practices Commission
to prepare this valuable and necessary document, we would be
happy to do so.

Please keep me informed of any plans to prepare such a manual.

Thank you for your consideration of this suggestion.

Sincerely,

Marie McKechnie
Marie McKechnie, CMC
President
City Clerk, City of
  Millbrae


/mm

Representing all City Clerks within the Counties of ALAMEDA, ALPINE, AMADOR, BUTTE, COLUSA, CONTRA COSTA, DEL NORTE, EL DORADO, GLENN,
HUMBOLDT, LAKE, LASSEN, MARIN, MENDOCINO, MODOC, NAPA, NEVADA, PLACER, PLUMAS, SACRAMENTO, SAN FRANCISCO, SAN MATEO,
SANTA CLARA, SANTA CRUZ, SHASTA, SIERRA, SISKIYOU, SOLANO, SONOMA, SUTTER, TEHAMA, TRINITY, YOLO and YUBA.

Northern California City Clerks' Association
A DIVISION OF THE CITY CLERKS ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA
REC 20 IU 48 AH '81

August 25, 1981


Mr. Michael Bennett
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento CA  95804

Dear Mr. Bennett:

At a recent meeting of the Northern California City Clerks'
Association, Ms. Nancy Anthony, City Clerk of Larkspur,
discussed her letter to Assemblyman Filante suggesting the
Fair Political Practices Commission prepare a manual covering
municipal functions for campaign reporting.

The member cities present unanimously supported that suggestion and directed that a letter indicating such support be
forwarded to you.

If there is any further information or comments we can provide
to you to encourage the Fair Political Practices Commission
to prepare this valuable and necessary document, we would be
happy to do so.

Please keep me informed of any plans to prepare such a manual.

Thank you for your consideration of this suggestion.

Sincerely,

Marie McKechnie
Marie McKechnie, CMC
President
City Clerk, City of
   Millbrae


/mm


Representing all City Clerks within the Counties of ALAMEDA, ALPINE, AMADOR, BUTTE, COLUSA, CONTRA COSTA, DEL NORTE, EL DORADO, GLENN,
HUMBOLDT, LAKE, LASSEN, MARIN, MENDOCINO, MODOC, NAPA, NEVADA, PLACER, PLUMAS, SACRAMENTO, SAN FRANCISCO, SAN MATEO,
